title: Expense Report Anomaly Detection
dataSources: Primary line-item feed: `index=business` with sourcetype `concur:expense:entries`, produced by a custom modular input or Splunk Add-on Builder Python worker on a Heavy Forwarder that obtains OAuth2 bearer tokens from SAP Concur (`POST https://us.api.concursolutions.com/oauth2/v0/token` or regional equivalent with `grant_type=client_credentials`, `client_id`, `client_secret`, `scope` including `EXP_RPT` / `RECEIPTS`), polls `GET https://www.concursolutions.com/api/v3.0/expense/entries` after resolving report IDs via `GET .../expense/reports`, and writes JSON lines at hourly grain with fields such as `TransactionDate`, `TransactionAmount`, `ExpenseTypeCode`, `VendorDescription`, `ReceiptImageStatus`, `OwnerLoginID`, plus HR-enriched `department` from Workday/SuccessFactors lookup or Concur user profile sync. Companion audit trail: `concur:audit` via `GET .../reportingauditlog/v1/auditLog`. Parallel SaaS paths (same index): Expensify Integration Server POST jobs landing `expensify:report` / `expensify:transaction`, with real-time `expensify.report.state.change` pushes through HTTP Event Collector token `hec_expensify_webhook` on the Splunk Cloud ACS input or Heavy Forwarder raw TCPâ†’HEC relay; Coupa REST approved-report pulls (`GET /api/expense_reports`) into `coupa:expense_report`; Workday calculated-field RaaS `Workday_Expense_Reports_For_Audit` JSON into `workday:expense:reports`. ERP substantiation for duplicate-detection joins and AP reconciliation: Splunk DB Connect (splunk_app_db_connect, Splunkbase 2686) JDBC batched reads projecting SAP FI vendor-clearing contexts (`BSAK`, `BSEG` expense postings where deployed), Oracle EBS `AP_EXPENSE_REPORT_HEADERS_ALL`, or similar mart tables into sourcetypes such as `dbx:ap_expense_lines` so Splunk correlates reimbursed invoice keys against SaaS export IDs.
app: Splunk DB Connect ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)), package `splunk_app_db_connect`, on a Heavy Forwarder or supported DB Connect worker for JDBC extraction from ERP AP tables / warehouse marts (`dbx:ap_expense_lines` deployment-defined sourcetype). Splunk HTTP Event Collector ([Splunk Docs â€” Use the HTTP Event Collector](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/Data/UsetheHTTPEventCollector)) enabled on Splunk Cloud or Enterprise with dedicated tokens for Expensify webhook callbacks and optional AI-partner payload sinks (ABBYY Timeline for Finance / AppZen Mastermind vendor APIs brokered through the same collector pattern when Finance Ops exposes CSV or JSON batches). Custom modular inputs built with Splunk Add-on Builder or plain Python polling SAP Concur REST (`api/v3.0/expense/entries`) â€” no Splunk-supported Concur TA ships canonical sourcetypes as of 2026; package naming stays tenant-specific (`TA_concur_expense_poll`). No Splunk Common Information Model dependency for this UC.
spl:
index=business sourcetype="concur:expense:entries" earliest=-90d
| eval TransactionDate_clean=replace(coalesce(TransactionDate,transaction_date,""),"Z","")
| eval td_epoch=coalesce(strptime(TransactionDate_clean,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3Q"),strptime(TransactionDate_clean,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"),strptime(TransactionDate_clean,"%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval transaction_dow=if(isnotnull(td_epoch),tonumber(strftime(td_epoch,"%u")),null())
| eval amount=tonumber(TransactionAmount)
| eval department=tostring(coalesce(department,DepartmentName,OrgUnit,"UNKNOWN"))
| eval anomaly_flags=mvappend(
    if(amount=round(amount,0) AND amount>=100 AND (amount=100 OR amount=200 OR amount=250 OR amount=500 OR amount=1000 OR amount=2500 OR amount=5000), "ROUND_NUMBER_SPLIT_RULE", null()),
    if((amount>=24 AND amount<25) OR (amount>=74 AND amount<75) OR (amount>=499 AND amount<500) OR (amount>=2499 AND amount<2500), "JUST_BELOW_APPROVAL_THRESHOLD", null()),
    if(transaction_dow>=6 AND ExpenseTypeCode IN ("ENT","MEAL","TRVL"), "WEEKEND_DISCRETIONARY_CATEGORY", null()),
    if(ReceiptImageStatus IN ("Missing","NotReceived") AND amount>25, "MISSING_RECEIPT_OVER_THRESHOLD", null()),
    if(match(VendorDescription,"(?i)(liquor|casino|adult|sportsbook|cannabis)"), "OUT_OF_POLICY_VENDOR", null()))
| eventstats avg(amount) as dept_avg, stdev(amount) as dept_stdev, count as dept_n by department, ExpenseTypeCode
| eval statistical_outlier=if(dept_n>=20 AND amount > dept_avg + 2*dept_stdev, "STATISTICAL_OUTLIER_2SIGMA", null())
| eval anomaly_flags=mvappend(anomaly_flags, statistical_outlier)
| where mvcount(anomaly_flags) > 0
| stats count as flagged_entries, sum(amount) as total_flagged_amount, values(anomaly_flags) as reasons, values(VendorDescription) as merchants by OwnerLoginID, department
| eventstats sum(total_flagged_amount) as portfolio_flagged
| eval pct_of_portfolio=round(100*total_flagged_amount/portfolio_flagged, 1)
| sort - total_flagged_amount
| table OwnerLoginID, department, flagged_entries, total_flagged_amount, pct_of_portfolio, reasons, merchants
description: Flags SAP Concur expense entry lines ingested into Splunk where rule-driven anomaly codes attach â€” Benford-adjacent round-dollar thresholds common to split-limit gaming, corridor amounts parked just below receipt or successive approval tiers, Saturday-or-Sunday discretionary expense-type postings when paired with audit-sensitive categories, missing receipt imagery beyond typical IRS-style substantiation floors when dollar amounts exceed twenty-five currency units, merchant-description keyword hits aligned to prohibited-spend categories, and two-sigma upward outliers versus peer averages inside each department-by-expense-type cohort â€” then aggregates flagged dollar exposure by submitting employee login so Finance receives a reimbursement-exception queue rather than reading full populations.
value: Accounts-payable and internal-audit teams concentrate scarce review hours on employee reimbursement outliers aligned with occupational-fraud statistics â€” ACFE Report to the Nations highlights travel-and-entertainment reimbursement schemes among frequent asset-misappropriation patterns with median fraud-loss figures material enough to impair earnings narratives â€” while COSO-style segregation-of-duties monitoring and Sarbanes-Oxley Section 404 expectations both reward continuous transaction surveilling rather than sampling alone; Splunk operationalizes those signals across SaaS expense SaaS feeds plus ERP JDBC corroboration so policy owners defend reimbursement controls before quarter attestations without treating customer collections dashboards that UC-23.6.1 targets.
implementation: Stand up OAuth-backed Concur polling into `index=business` / `concur:expense:entries`, bind HR `department`, enable HEC for Expensify webhooks where deployed, add DB Connect JDBC slices over AP mart tables for duplicate-payment joins, normalize currency columns before thresholds, save this SPL as `finance_te_anomaly_review_queue`, schedule weekly or nightly over `-90d`, route CSV digest to Controller mailbox rather than Sev1 paging.
visualization: Dashboard Studio table ranked by `total_flagged_amount` showing `OwnerLoginID`, `department`, `pct_of_portfolio`, and multi-value `reasons` (top-flagged employees triage queue); stacked column chart counting anomaly-flag hits stacked by decoded reason codes across the fleet (ROUND_NUMBER_SPLIT_RULE versus JUST_BELOW_APPROVAL_THRESHOLD breakdown); matrix-style heatmap faceting anomaly reason against `department` for hotspot geography; single-value visualization pair charting percentage of reimbursed dollars touching any anomaly flag plus twenty-eight-day sparkline trend from summary-index acceleration of the nightly run; optional Sankey diagram flowing anomaly-type buckets into disposition buckets (`confirmed_policy_exception`, `escalated_hotline`, `cleared_false_positive`) sourced from manual KV-store lookups Finance annotates after review.

references.title: Splunk Docs â€” Deploy and use DB Connect
COSO â€” Internal Control and Fraud Deterrence (framework home)
ACFE â€” Fraud resources (includes Report to the Nations research)
IRS Publication 463 (Travel, Gift, and Car Expenses)
SAP Concur Developer Center â€” API Reference

knownFalsePositives: Sales kickoffs and distributor conferences routinely produce legitimate Saturday-night entertainment rows flagged by WEEKEND_DISCRETIONARY_CATEGORY despite approved agendas filed outside Splunk. International travelers submitting EUR-equivalent rows against USD approval thresholds appear as JUST_BELOW_APPROVAL_THRESHOLD noise until treasury publishes FX rounding rules beside Splunk thresholds. Tokyo or Manhattan meal totals clustering seventy-three to seventy-four dollars reflect policy-compliant high-cost-locality per-diem bands rather than receipt-evasion â€” geography lookups missing from Concur profiles amplify this. Duplicate-detection logic colliding with shared corporate-card merchant descriptors across unrelated teammates during conference hotel clusters raises collisions until AP passes canonical ERP invoice keys through DB Connect joins. Round-dollar taxi vouchers at exactly two hundred fifty dollars after vendor rounding frequently resemble ROUND_NUMBER_SPLIT_RULE without intent. Insight Premium or AppZen may already reroute severe offenders inside Concur workflows while Splunk still mirrors residual narrative rows â€” reviewers interpret Splunk hits as backlog hygiene signals, not standalone accusations.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
â€˘ Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search tier aligned with splunkVersions; Heavy Forwarder or container worker hosting Splunk DB Connect alongside outbound HTTPS allowances toward SAP Concur API endpoints plus JDBC connectivity into Oracle AP reporting schemas or SAP-supported warehouse replicas Finance sanctions.
â€˘ Splunk roles restricting reimbursement anomaly outputs to Finance investigations, Internal Audit, and Controller delegates â€” avoid surfacing HR disciplinary conclusions solely from Splunk dashboards.
â€˘ Corporate travel policy artifact keyed by effective dates (`policy_version`, receipt thresholds, FX rounding tables) maintained outside Splunk yet referenced via lookups referenced inside SPL macros.
â€˘ Splunk DB Connect identities granted read-only JDBC across curated expense mart projections (`vw_ap_expense_lines`) tied to ERP posting batches employees ultimately recognize as reimbursement cash movements.

Step 1 â€” Configure data collection
Install Splunk DB Connect from Splunkbase 2686 on the Heavy Forwarder bundle hosting JDBC workloads; register Oracle JDBC (`ojdbc11.jar`) or SAP HANA (`ngdbc.jar`) drivers consistent with ERP vendor contracts.

Provision OAuth credentials inside Secret Storage or Splunk credential overlays bound to custom modular inputs polling SAP Concur Expense APIs â€” authenticate against regional hosts (`POST /oauth2/v0/token`) capturing scopes covering expense reporting (`EXP_RPT`) plus receipt retrieval (`RECEIPTS`). Implement exponential backoff honoring Concur thirty-requests-per-minute guidance documented by SAP developer outreach.

Emit normalized JSON events into `index=business` sourcetype `concur:expense:entries`, stamping `_time` from `TransactionDate` where authoritative rather than indexer receipt latency.

Stand complementary JDBC inputs emitting `dbx:ap_expense_lines` rows keyed by cleared ERP voucher identifiers (`belnr`, `buzei`, Oracle `invoice_id`) so duplicate-detection SPL extensions join SaaS merchant descriptions against cleared postings.

Enable Splunk HTTP Event Collector tokens scoped for Expensify Integration Server webhook deliveries (`expensify.report.state.change`) landing sourcetype `expensify:report`, forwarding TLS-terminated payloads through Splunk Cloud ACS routing rules where Finance forbids Heavy Forwarder hops.

Step 2 â€” Create the search and alert
Paste the SPL from the parent `spl` field into Search & Reporting; macro-wrap threshold literals (`threshold_low_receipt`, `threshold_vp_cap`) inside `macros.conf` so Corporate Accounting adjusts corridor widths without rewriting SPL fragments.

Save as scheduled report `finance_te_anomaly_weekly`; attach alert actions emailing CSV extracts through scripted alerts â€” throttle per `OwnerLoginID` across seventy-two hours because benign travelers accumulate repetitive grocery receipts flagged multiple nights.

Step 3 â€” Mechanics auditors care about
Concur expense entries arrive pre-exploded into vendor-facing dimensions Finance recognizes (`ExpenseTypeCode`, `ReceiptImageStatus`), letting Splunk attach deterministic anomaly codes without rebuilding OCR pipelines locally.

`transaction_dow` interprets ISO weekday digits Monday-through-Sunday (`strftime(... "%u")`) so Saturday equals six â€” discretionary categories layered atop weekends behave distinctly from weekday commuter meals sharing identical merchants.

`eventstats` calculates cohort averages strictly inside `(department, ExpenseTypeCode)` buckets â€” tightening cohort scope avoids comparing airfare magnitudes against taxi vouchers absent HR diligence.

The trailing `stats` block collapses noisy row-level ledger noise into reimbursement-owner summaries suited for managerial escalation workflows synchronized with HR confidentiality norms.

Step 4 â€” Operationalize and validate
Publish a Dashboard Studio workbook (`Finance_TE_Anomaly_Review`) pinned to Controller and Travel Risk stewards with row-level CSV export restricted via Splunk role maps â€” default cadence remains weekly Monday reviews rather than paging duty rotations.

Route scheduled-report alert actions delivering gzip CSV attachments through authenticated SMTP relays into the shared mailbox finance-te-review@example.com with retention aligned to HR investigation policies rather than immutable SIEM buckets.

Maintain `te_review_disposition.csv` KV lookups edited only by audit managers so downstream Sankey panels join anomaly codes to disposition outcomes without rewriting SPL skeletons weekly.

Pick five flagged rows inside Splunk; locate identical expense artifacts inside SAP Concur Expense workspace URLs referenced through deep-link macros embedding `ReportID` plus `EntryID`.

Cross-foot summed flagged reimbursement totals versus Controller-certified expense-aging extracts pulled straight from ERP AP modules DB Connect mirrors â€” tolerate rounding deltas smaller than ledger posting tolerance documented under corporate accounting policies.

Compare anomaly percentages versus Internal Audit sampling schedules referenced during Sarbanes-Oxley Section 404 testing narratives.

Step 5 â€” Troubleshooting
â€˘ SAP Concur Insights Premium plus AppZen Mastermind frequently reroutes obviously abusive submissions inside vendor workflows before Splunk ingestion mirrors JSON payloads â€” downstream Splunk dashboards under-count headline fraud yet remain vital for residue-pattern hunting AppZen deliberately ignores.
â€˘ Expensify SmartScan OCR occasionally mistranscribes handwritten totals (`USD 50.00` versus `USD 49.99`), falsely triggering JUST_BELOW_APPROVAL_THRESHOLD until processors manually rewrite digits.
â€˘ Concur receipt-upload UX delays flipping `ReceiptImageStatus` from Missing to Received up to forty-eight hours while travelers sync mobile uploads â€” MISSING_RECEIPT_OVER_THRESHOLD clears automatically after nightly reconciliation reruns.
â€˘ Travel-heavy metro assignments legitimately crowd seventy-four-dollar meals beneath IRS-style receipt thresholds despite appearances of corridor stacking â€” layering geo lookups resolves conflicts absent blunt suppression.
â€˘ Currency pipelines mixing USD-approved totals against EUR-line postings exaggerate corridor breaches whenever treasury publishes averaged ECB rates asynchronously versus card-authorization timestamps Splunk snapshots capture.
â€˘ Statistical cohort counts (`dept_n`) smaller than twenty intentionally suppress variance math â€” rotational hires joining departments mid-quarter inherit unstable denominators until cohort maturity returns.
â€˘ Travel-policy revisions shifting seventy-five-dollar receipt floors upward mid-quarter orphan legacy Splunk thresholds referencing stale macros unless governance publishes simultaneous macro edits tied to effective dating controls.
â€˘ Employees splitting cost-center allocations across departments distort peer-average integrity unless Splunk adopts weighted-average attribution sourced from HR hierarchy feeds refreshed nightly.
â€˘ AI-centric vendors removing obviously fraudulent receipts upstream shrink Splunk-visible severity distributions yet amplify residual long-tail ambiguity investigators still adjudicate manually.
â€˘ Quarterly Sarbanes-Oxley-testing mandates auditing every reimbursement packet briefly overwhelming Splunk-derived queues despite dashboards emphasizing statistically prioritized subsets.
â€˘ Splunk DB Connect JDBC pulls capped below vendor-imposed fetch-row ceilings occasionally truncate intra-day batches â€” reconcile watermark checkpoints whenever Oracle listeners emit fetch-aborted warnings referencing incomplete vouchers.
grandmaExplanation: We scan employee expense receipts for patterns that look like cheating or mistakes â€” duplicate dinners, sneaky amounts just under approval limits, or weekends charged like weekdays â€” so Finance reviews a short suspicious list instead of every reimbursement request coming through payroll.
